validation_criteria: make bundle-size shows delta < 30 KB after the slice merges
git diff frontend/package.json is empty after the slice
no shared Modal primitive extracted (each manager owns its modal)

propagation_effects.note: Reaffirmed as the load-bearing budget; incremental measurement after each component
B1 may need scope cuts if budget tightens; least-essential surface first (likely MCP setup panel)

validation_criteria: After modal mount, the plaintext element has CSS filter: blur(...) via a CSS class
Click on the plaintext or the Reveal button toggles the blur class off
Copy-to-clipboard returns the unobscured plaintext regardless of blur state (Playwright assertion)
After modal unmount, the bound plaintext state is empty and not retained anywhere in the component tree

propagation_effects.note: Acknowledgment-gate semantics preserved; blur is orthogonal

tensions.failure_cascade: If click-to-reveal handler fails, blurred plaintext is still copyable via the Copy button (independent of reveal state). Worst case: admin can't read the value but can still copy/paste. Acceptable degradation.

validation_criteria: Approve handler accesses response.json() on at most one code path (the success branch)
Error rendering renders only HTTP status + trimmed error.message; never the raw response
e2e test: synthetic 500 from approve does not produce 'phr_live_' substring in DOM
e2e test: synthetic 201 from approve does not invoke console.* with the plaintext (Playwright console capture)

propagation_effects.note: Error rendering for credential-bearing endpoints must use the structured wrapper, not raw response
Explicit code-path discipline localised to a typed wrapper helper

evidence.test_name: approve form collects scope and label
workspace rename updates display name; slug stays read-only
MCP setup copy buttons fire clipboard with expected URLs
plaintext modal full lifecycle (mount, blur, reveal, copy, ack, unmount-clear)
approve flow console capture detects no phr_live_ leak
approve flow leaves localStorage / sessionStorage / IndexedDB unchanged

binding_constraint.reason: Binds three regulation-tagged constraints (U1, S1, plus the on-unmount clear that downstream supports S3) and the cross-cutting TN2 resolution. Most complex single artifact; if it ships wrong the whole feature is unsafe regardless of how clean the other surfaces look. Verification difficulty highest: on-unmount state-clear and storage-leak assertions are structural properties that can't be eyeballed.

tension_validation.note: Option A reuses existing manager modal patterns; no shared primitive. Bundle delta estimated ~10-15 KB << 30 KB.
RT-1 codifies the blur + reveal toggle and acknowledgment gate.
RT-2 codifies the mintApiKey() typed wrapper with single-response-read discipline.

draft_required_truths.draft_statement: A blocking modal component exists that gates dismiss on an explicit acknowledgment checkbox AND clears its plaintext-bound state on unmount.
The workspace surface exposes display-name editing via PATCH but no slug-edit affordance.
An MCP setup panel exists, populated from /api/session or a new /api/oauth/discovery endpoint that surfaces the issuer + JWKS URLs.
Bundle-size CI gate runs on every change; baseline is updated only when explicitly justified.
Playwright e2e covers plaintext modal acknowledgment gate, approve-form fields, workspace rename, and MCP setup copy-button feedback.

gap_checklist_compliance.note: Core data path: admin click -> /api/admin/* -> response -> refetch -> render. Each transition crosses the HTTP boundary; integration tests live at the API layer (Stage 1-3 coverage) and the UX layer (this manifold's O1).
S1 + S3 cover the auth attack surface for the once-shown plaintext: storage exfil, console leak. Server-side crypto attack matrix already covered by user-mgmt-mcp Stage 1c.

gap_checklist_compliance.skip_reason: No new unauthenticated endpoints; admin UI sits behind cookie session + withAdmin gate.

gap_checklist_compliance.note: Approve-form input (scope, label, expires_at) validates client-side as well as server-side. Workspace name input trimmed + non-empty checked.

gap_checklist_compliance.skip_reason: No new shared mutable state introduced. Each modal owns its own component-scoped state.

iterations.result: Discovered 16 constraints across 5 categories. Scope ratified: four concrete gaps from m6-style codebase audit (plaintext modal, workspace rename display-name only, OAuth/MCP discovery URLs, approve-form fields). Speculative additions deferred. Pre-mortem surfaced B3 (workspace slug immutable) and S3 (plaintext-never-in-console).

Found 3 tensions, all resolved. TN1 (B1 × T3): bundle-budget pressure resolved by reusing existing manager modal patterns + incremental bundle-size measurement after each component (no shared Modal primitive — abstraction would cost more than it saves at 3 instances). TN2 (U1 × S1): shoulder-surfing exposure resolved by click-to-reveal blur (industry convention: 1Password / AWS / GitHub PATs); preserves U1's acknowledgment-gate while limiting ambient exposure. TN3 (U4 × S3): error-surface plaintext-leak risk resolved via P1+P10+P24 — typed mintApiKey() wrapper localises the discipline so future maintainers can't accidentally regress.

Derived 10 required truths covering 5 component artifacts (RT-1 plaintext modal, RT-2 mintKey wrapper, RT-3 approve form, RT-4 workspace rename, RT-5 MCP setup panel), 1 routing wire (RT-6 palette + App.svelte), 1 e2e contract (RT-7), 2 CI gates (RT-8 bundle, RT-9 deps), and 1 storage-cleanliness assertion (RT-10). Binding constraint: RT-1 (the plaintext modal — binds 3 regulation-tagged constraints + TN2 resolution, and is the structurally hardest verification surface). Recommended Option A (surgical patch, ~10-15 KB bundle delta, TWO_WAY); Option B (shared Modal primitive) would REOPEN TN1; Option C (admin-as-SPA) would VIOLATE TN1 and TN3 — DO NOT PURSUE. All 3 m2 tensions CONFIRMED under Option A.

m4-generate Option A (surgical patch). Binding-RT-first generation: PlaintextModal.svelte (RT-1, 226 lines) + mintKey.ts (RT-2, 106 lines) → KeysManager.svelte edits (form fields RT-3 + modal mount RT-1 + wrapper use RT-2) → WorkspaceManager.svelte inline rename (RT-4 + B3 slug-locked) → MCPSetupPanel.svelte (RT-5, 286 lines) → CommandPalette.svelte + App.svelte routing (RT-6) → e2e specs (RT-7). Bundle delta gzipped: +3.73 KB (203.05 → 206.78), well under T3's 30 KB ceiling. Hand-rolled — zero new npm deps (RT-9). 4 new files, 4 modified, 3 new test files.
satisfies: RT-1 (binding — plaintext modal with blur/reveal + ack gate)
RT-7 (e2e: plaintext modal lifecycle + console-silence + workspace rename + MCP copy)
tests_added: approve flow: form fields, plaintext modal lifecycle, ack-gate, on-dismiss clear
plaintext modal: Escape key is suppressed until ack
approve flow: error path renders structured message; no plaintext in console
approve form: rejects empty label client-side
discovery + JWKS URLs render with copy buttons; copy fires clipboard with the right value
OAuth not configured: panel shows the env-var helper instead of broken URLs
default workspace can be renamed; slug stays read-only
cancel rename leaves the workspace untouched

iterations.result: m5-verify pass with bg e2e completed. 19 of 21 admin/ tests green; 2 failing in workspace-rename.spec.ts on a palette-typing race (helper skips await-placeholder-visible before keyboard.type). 13 SATISFIED, 3 PARTIAL, 0 DEFERRED of 16. Binding RT-1 SATISFIED at TESTED — plaintext modal lifecycle + Escape-suppression + storage-cleanliness all green. RT-4 + B1 + B3 + O1 stuck at PARTIAL because the 2 workspace-rename tests can't reach their assertions. 3 tensions confirmed; 0 reopened. Bundle delta +3.73 KB << 30 KB.
iterations.convergence_blocked_on: G1: workspace-rename palette race — 2 e2e tests failing

iterations.result: Post-G1 fix: 21/21 admin/ tests green in 5.2s. Two test-helper edits closed G1 — (a) await-placeholder-visible defensive addition, (b) the actual root cause: scope palette-item locator to .palette-item to disambiguate from the TopBar workspace-switcher's hidden <option>Manage workspaces…</option>. The text collision was specific to 'Manage workspaces' (workspace switcher option) — 'Manage users' and 'Connect an MCP client' have no <option> twins. Manifold CONVERGED: 16/16 constraints SATISFIED, 10/10 RTs at TESTED, binding RT-1 closed, 0 gaps. 3 tensions confirmed; 0 reopened. Bundle delta +3.73 KB << 30 KB.
